zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster於1332年7月6日出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 安特里姆卡利福格斯 卡素時，她的父親William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster19歲，母親Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster23歲。她於1352年8月15日與Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence在英國米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1363年12月10日在愛爾蘭庫迪 度布林度布林享年31歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可克萊爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster于1332年7月6日出生在爱尔兰庫迪 安特里姆卡利福格斯 卡素时，她的父亲William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster19岁，母亲Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster23岁。她于1352年8月15日与Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence在英国米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1363年12月10日在爱尔兰庫迪 度布林度布林享年31岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可克萊爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster was born on 6 July 1332, in Carrickfergus Castle, County Antrim, Ireland, her father, William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster, was 19 and her mother, Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster, was 23. She married Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence on 15 August 1352, in Tower of London, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 10 December 1363, in Dublin, County Dublin, Ireland, at the age of 31, and was buried in Clare, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster est née le 6 juillet 1332, à Carrickfergus Castle, County Antrim, Irlande, son père, William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence le 15 août 1352, à Tower of London, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 10 décembre 1363, à Dublin, County Dublin, Irlande, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterrée à Clare, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster am 6. Juli 1332 in Carrickfergus Castle, County Antrim, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster 19 und ihre Mutter Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster 23. Sie hat am 15. August 1352 in Tower of London, Middlesex, England Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 10. Dezember 1363 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Dublin, County Dublin, Irland und wurde in Clare, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster è nata il il 6 luglio 1332 nel Carrickfergus Castle, County Antrim, Irlanda, sua padre, William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence il 15 agosto 1352, nel Tower of London, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 10 dicembre 1363, nel Dublin, County Dublin, Irlanda, all'età di 31 anni, e fu sepolta a Clare, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulsterが1332年7月6日にアイルランドコウントイ アントリムカーリクフエルグス カストルで生まれた時，父William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulsterは19歳，母Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulsterは23歳であった。1352年8月15日にイギリスミッドレセックストウエル オフ ロンドンでLionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarenceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1363年12月10日にアイルランドコウントイ ダブリンダブリンで31歳で死去し，イギリススフォルククラーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster(이)가 1332년 7월 6일 아일랜드 카운티 앤트림 카릭퍼구스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1352년 8월 15일 영국 미들레세스 타워 프 런던에서 Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1363년 12월 10일 아일랜드 카운티 드블린 드블린에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 클래르에 묻혔다.

pt.Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, Engla: Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster nasceu em 6 de julho de 1332, em Carrickfergus Castle, County Antrim, Irlanda, seu pai, William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence em 15 de agosto de 1352, em Tower of London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 10 de dezembro de 1363, em Dublin, County Dublin, Irlanda, com 31 anos, e foi sepultada em Clare, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, Engla: стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster родилась 6 июля 1332 г. в местности карриккфиргус кастл, коунти антрим, Ирландия, её отцу по имени William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence 15 августа 1352 г. в местности тоуир оф лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 10 декабря 1363 г. в местности дублинь, коунти дублинь, Ирландия в возрасте 31 год и была похоронена в местности клар, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster nació el 6 de julio de 1332, en Carrickfergus Castle, County Antrim, Irlanda, su papá, William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Lionel of Antwerp 1st Duke of Clarence el 15 de agosto de 1352, en Tower of London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 10 de diciembre de 1363, en Dublin, County Dublin, Irlanda, a la edad de 31 años, y fue sepultada en Clare, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
